behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from recommendation results regardless of relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between inventory-availability filtering and ranking/result assembly. MOCK-MISS: this failure appears when high-scoring ranked items are merged with availability flags, not just in a single pure function. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no real OS/network/model judgment required. Rejection-gate: System is rejected because behavior text only requires catalog+availability logic, not real external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire recommendation service + availability filter + ranking module with an in-process catalog fixture containing high-score out-of-stock items. Assert final page contains zero out-of-stock IDs even when they would otherwise rank first.

behaviors.description: User preference filter excludes categories explicitly blocked in settings.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between user-settings retrieval and category filter application. MOCK-MISS: bug is often in handoff (settings format, missing join, stale mapping), not isolated predicate logic. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no OS/network/model dependence required. Rejection-gate: System disqualified because behavior does not require real remote settings service; local wiring is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommender with in-process user-profile/settings module and product catalog spanning blocked and allowed categories. Verify blocked categories are absent in output while allowed categories remain.

behaviors.description: Products matching recent browsing patterns rank higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: local function/object seam in scoring/ranking logic. MOCK-MISS: behavior is an algorithmic weighting rule that can fail inside scorer logic without module interaction. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no real infra and no nondeterministic model output. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because behavior text is about score ordering by browsing signal, which can be validated directly in scorer inputs/outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke ranking/scoring function with controlled feature vectors where only browsing-signal differs. Assert items with stronger recent-browse features receive strictly higher score/rank than otherwise equivalent items.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns correct page with stable ordering; requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if data unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between rank ordering and pagination slice logic. MOCK-MISS: repeated-request stability issues usually come from interaction (tie-breaking, sort keys, pagination offsets). REAL-INFRA: no required real network/OS primitive. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System rejected because determinism here is intra-service ordering/slicing, reproducible in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full in-process recommendation pipeline twice with identical snapshot data and same page params. Assert page boundaries are correct and page-2 item IDs/order are identical across both calls.

behaviors.description: Algorithm surfaces at least one product from an unseen category (serendipity) to avoid filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between core ranker and diversity/serendipity constraint stage. MOCK-MISS: failure emerges when diversification is applied (or skipped) after ranking. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no external infra or model-judgment dependency. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because this is deterministic business-rule diversity, not subjective LLM quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommender pipeline with user history covering categories A/B and candidate pool containing A/B/C. Verify top-N includes at least one category outside browsed set while still respecting other hard filters.

behaviors.description: New user with no purchase/browsing history gets popularity-based recommendations, not empty results.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between cold-start detection and popularity fallback provider. MOCK-MISS: bug is commonly in routing path selection and fallback wiring. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior does not require real external analytics service; local popularity fixture can expose failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create user state with empty history and run end-to-end in-process recommendation call with populated popularity table. Assert non-empty output and that ranked IDs follow popularity ordering baseline.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records correct product ID, list position, and timestamp for each clicked recommendation.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between recommendation response metadata and click-event persistence. MOCK-MISS: mapping bugs (wrong position/id, timestamp field loss) appear during event construction + repository write. REAL-INFRA: local DB/file sink is enough; no real external infra required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text does not require real streaming bus/network semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Serve recommendations, simulate click on a known position, then read stored tracking record from local persistence adapter. Assert exact product_id, 1-based/0-based position contract, and timestamp presence/format within expected clock window.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: environment seam (runtime performance on real process/OS scheduling/memory behavior). REAL-INFRA: yes; in-process micro-mocks hide latency from real data structures, allocator, and execution environment. MOCK-HIDE: stubs can pass while real workload exceeds SLA. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because SLA claim is wall-clock behavior under realistic load, which needs system-level execution.
behaviors.test_description: Run a system performance test against the actual recommendation binary/service in a production-like runtime with a real 100k-product dataset fixture; measure end-to-end request latency and assert p95 (or specified metric) <200ms.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased, algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between purchase-event ingestion/state update and ranking features. MOCK-MISS: failure is typically propagation lag or wrong feature update across modules. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no required external infra or agentic model. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because this is not multi-agentic sequencing; it is deterministic state transition plus rerank.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations, mark one recommended item as purchased via purchase-update path, then request recommendations again with unchanged other state. Assert purchased item rank drops below prior position (or below configured threshold).

behaviors.description: Results are deterministic for same user state; two requests with no intervening activity return same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam across feature assembly, ranking, and final sorting/tie-break pipeline. MOCK-MISS: non-determinism often appears from interaction (unstable sort, unordered map iteration, clock/random leakage). REAL-INFRA: no mandatory external OS/network primitive. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model-judgment dependency. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because behavior is deterministic ranking logic, not variable AI output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Call full in-process recommendation pipeline twice with identical user snapshot, fixed seed/clock where applicable, and unchanged catalog. Assert full ranked ID list equality across both calls.
